questions.question: Define Pushdown Automaton and list its components.
keyPoints: PDA is finite automaton with stack memory
Six components: Q (states), ő£ (input alphabet), őď (stack alphabet), őī (transition function), q‚āÄ (start state), F (accepting states)
Stack allows LIFO operations enabling recognition of CFLs

questions.question: Explain the two methods of acceptance in PDA with examples.
keyPoints: Acceptance by final state: Accept if ends in accepting state (any stack)
Acceptance by empty stack: Accept if stack becomes empty (any state)
Example: L = {0‚ĀŅ1‚ĀŅ} - by empty stack, push all 0s then pop for each 1
Both methods are equivalent in recognizing CFLs

questions.question: What is a configuration (Instantaneous Description) in PDA?
keyPoints: Configuration is triplet (q, w, ő≥) where q=state, w=remaining input, ő≥=stack contents
Initial configuration for input w: (q‚āÄ, w, Z‚āÄ)
Transition relation: (q, aw, Xő≤) ‚äĘ (p, w, őĪő≤) when őī(q, a, X) contains (p, őĪ)

questions.question: Differentiate between Deterministic PDA (DPDA) and Non-deterministic PDA (NPDA).
keyPoints: DPDA: őī(q, a, X) has at most one element - one move per configuration
NPDA: őī(q, a, X) can have multiple elements - explores multiple paths
DPDA recognizes deterministic CFLs, NPDA recognizes all CFLs
Every DPDA is also an NPDA, but not vice versa

questions.question: Show how PDA recognizes the language L = {ww^R | w ‚ąą {0,1}*}
keyPoints: Read input symbol by symbol
Push each symbol onto stack (0‚Üí0, 1‚Üí1)
At middle (guess), start popping
For each input symbol, pop matching stack symbol
Accept if stack empty at end

questions.question: Define context-free language and give examples of languages recognized by PDA.
keyPoints: CFL: Language generated by context-free grammar
Includes all regular languages plus more
Examples: L = {0‚ĀŅ1‚ĀŅ | n‚Č•0}, palindromes {ww^R}, balanced parentheses
Not all CFLs: {a‚ĀŅb‚ĀŅc‚ĀŅ} is not context-free

questions.question: Explain the equivalence of acceptance by empty stack and acceptance by final state.
keyPoints: Both methods recognize same language class (CFLs)
Empty stack to final state: Add new accepting state, push marker, accept when marker reached
Final state to empty stack: Add transitions to empty stack at accepting states
Theorem: For any PDA M‚āĀ accepting L by final state, there exists M‚āā accepting L by empty stack

questions.question: Design a PDA for the language L = {0‚ĀŅ1‚ĀŅ | n ‚Č• 1} showing all components and transitions.
keyPoints: Components: Q={q‚āÄ,q‚āĀ,q‚āā}, ő£={0,1}, őď={0,Z‚āÄ}, őī defined
Initial: (q‚āÄ, Z‚āÄ), Push 0 on each 0 in q‚āÄ‚Üíq‚āĀ
Transition to q‚āĀ on first 1, pop 0 for each 1
Accept by empty stack in q‚āā when input exhausted
Give transition table or diagram
Trace: 0011 ‚Üí (q‚āÄ,0011,Z‚āÄ) ‚äĘ (q‚āĀ,011,0Z‚āÄ) ‚äĘ (q‚āĀ,11,00Z‚āÄ) ‚äĘ (q‚āā,1,0Z‚āÄ) ‚äĘ (q‚āā,őĶ,Z‚āÄ) ACCEPT

questions.question: Construct a PDA for accepting set of all strings over {0,1} that are palindromes of even length.
keyPoints: Palindrome: w = w^R
Strategy: Push first half, compare second half by popping
Use non-deterministic choice to guess middle
Transitions: q‚āÄ: read 0/1 ‚Üí push same, stay in q‚āÄ
At any point guess őĶ-transition to q‚āĀ
In q‚āĀ: read symbol = pop matching symbol
Accept by empty stack in q‚āā
Show sample trace for 01001010

questions.question: Explain the Pumping Lemma for Context-Free Languages. Show that L = {a‚ĀŅb‚ĀŅc‚ĀŅ | n ‚Č• 1} is not context-free.
keyPoints: For CFL L, exists pumping length p such that any w‚ąąL with |w|‚Č•p can be written as uvxyz with conditions: |vxy|‚Č§p, |vy|‚Č•1, uv‚ĀĪxy‚ĀĪz‚ąąL for i‚Č•0
For L = {a‚ĀŅb‚ĀŅc‚ĀŅ}, assume CFL, let p be pumping length
Consider string s = aŠĶĖbŠĶĖcŠĶĖ, decompose s=uvxyz with |vxy|‚Č§p
v and y can only be from one or two types of symbols
If v or y contains mixed symbols, pumping creates disorder
If all from same type, pumping upsets equal counts
Contradiction: s not in L after pumping
Therefore L is not CFL

questions.question: Design a PDA with final state acceptance for L = {a¬≤‚ĀŅb‚ĀŅ | n ‚Č• 1}
keyPoints: Language: even number of a's followed by equal number of b's
Components: Q={q‚āÄ,q‚āĀ,q‚āā}, ő£={a,b}, őď={a,Z‚āÄ}
In q‚āÄ: read 'a' and push two 'a's (or push a and stay, push a again)
Count a's by pushing 2a per transition or use two moves
Move to q‚āĀ on first b
In q‚āĀ: pop one a for each b
Accept in q‚āā when stack has only Z‚āÄ
Alternate: Use őĶ-transition after processing all a's to enter checking state
